text: The Latin here describes God as filling Juda with ‘humiliated men and humiliated women’; the sense of the Hebrew is rather ‘lamentation and lament’, as in Is. 29.2.
Literally, ‘my liver is poured out on the ground’.
The Hebrew letters Pe and Ain are unaccountably transposed, here and in 3.46-51; 4.16 and 17.
The first part of this verse is perhaps corrupt; the Hebrew text of it gives a rather improbable sense.
Some think that we should interpret the Hebrew text as meaning ‘(some) of my neighbours’, not ‘terrors’.
